ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CCSF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND DECLATORY RELIEF RE: DUTY TO DEFEND.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON ITS THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND DECLARATORY RELIEF FOR A DUTY TO DEFEND AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT THYSSENKRUPP ELEVATOR AMERICA IS GRANTED. BASED ON THE UNDISPUTED FACTS, THE CITY IS ENTITLED TO AN ORDER THAT THYSSENKRUPP IS OBLIGATED TO DEFEND IT AGAINST THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN OSWALD IN THIS CASE. PER THE DUTY TO DEFEND LANGUAGE IN THE PARTIES' CONTRACT AND CRAWFORD WEATHER SHIELD MANUFACTURING, INC. (2008) 44 CAL. 44 541, THYSSENKRUPP BECAME OBLIGATED TO DEFEND THE CITY AGAINST THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY MS. OSWALD IN THIS CASE ONCE THE CITY MADE A DEMAND THAT IT BE DEFENDED. ALL OF THYSSENKRUPP'S PROCEDURAL ARGUMENTS LACK MERIT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600 =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Third Cause Of Action For Breach Of Contract E: Failure To Defend And Fifth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief Re: Duty To Defend
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Anna T. Pham of the Leclair Ryan, Tel. No. 1-415-913-4948 appearing
for the Defendant;
Atty. Christopher Olsen of Clinton & Clinton, Tel. No. 1-562-216-5000
appearing for the Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator by Telephone appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-complainant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication on its third and fifth causes of action for breach of contract and declaratory relief for a duty to defend against cross-defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator America is granted. Based on the undisputed facts, the City is entitled to an order that Thyssenkrupp is obligated to defend it against the claims alleged by plaintiff Kathleen Oswald in this case. Per the duty to defend language in the parties' contract and Crawford Weather Shield Manufacturing, Inc. (2008) 44 Cal. 44 541, Thyssenkrupp became obligated to defend the City against the claims alleged by Ms. Oswald in this case once the City made a demand that it be defended. All of Thyssenkrupp's procedural arguments lack merit. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
